title: Server Won't Start: Disk Full
description:
## The Situation

Your on-call alert fires at 2am. The application server is down and won't restart. The error is cryptic: `No space left on device`.

Log rotation was supposed to clean up old logs automatically, but something broke last month and compressed log archives have been piling up in `/var/log/app/`.

The server startup script refuses to launch if disk usage in `/var/log/app/` exceeds **200MB**. You need to clean it up.

## Your Task

1. Investigate what's taking up space
2. Identify and remove old log archives
3. Confirm the server can start

## Useful Commands

```bash
# Check what's taking up space
du -sh /var/log/app/*
du -sh /var/log/app/

# List files by size
ls -lhS /var/log/app/

# Find large files
find /var/log/app -name '*.gz' -size +10M

# Try to start the server (currently fails)
/home/user/start_server.sh

# Delete files older than 30 days (example)
find /var/log/app -name '*.gz' -mtime +30 -delete
```
hints: Run du -sh /var/log/app/* to see which files are taking up the most space.
The compressed log archives (*.gz files) from previous months are the culprit. Old logs don't need to be kept.
Delete the old .gz files from /var/log/app/ using rm or find -delete, then run /home/user/start_server.sh to verify.
